_meta.description: AIWIZN scenario variant pool. 39 variants across 13 nodes. v6.3.0 corrects sub-node alignment between variant content and the actual choice text in the app.
_meta.author: AIWIZN / ATEN Inc 2026

s1a.label: Anterolateral STEMI - hypotensive
s1a.env_title: Mr. David Osei, 58M - 'It is just indigestion'
s1a.env_meta: 15 minutes ago he walked in. Now on the monitor: ST elevation, chest pressure radiating to jaw, diaphoresis.
s1a.ecg_banner: WARNING ST ELEVATION . V2-V5 . STEMI PROTOCOL ACTIVE

s1a.label: Inferior STEMI with RV involvement
s1a.env_title: Mr. Walker Hayes, 64M - 'It feels like an elephant on my chest'
s1a.env_meta: Brought in by paramedics. Pain onset 35 minutes ago while shovelling snow. Diaphoretic, anxious, pointing at sternum.
s1a.ecg_banner: WARNING ST ELEVATION . II/III/aVF . INFERIOR STEMI . RV INVOLVEMENT LIKELY

s1a.label: Silent MI - atypical diabetic presentation
s1a.env_title: Mr. Theodore Okonkwo, 71M - 'Just a bit of indigestion'
s1a.env_meta: Type 2 diabetic, walked into triage 90 minutes after symptom onset. No chest pain. Subtle Q waves on ECG. Wife noticed clamminess in the car.
s1a.ecg_banner: WARNING ST DEPRESSION V4-V6 . NEW Q WAVES . SILENT MI SUSPECTED

s1a2.label: SBAR to interventionalist - inferior STEMI
s1a2.env_label: Scenario 1-A handover . phone to cath lab
s1a2.env_title: Dr. Marquez scrubbing - 30 seconds to land SBAR
s1a2.env_meta: You have the interventional cardiologist on the line. She is gowning. Sixty seconds before she goes scrub side. Your SBAR has to be tight.
s1a2.ecg_banner: INFERIOR STEMI . CATH READY . VERBAL ORDER NEEDED

s1a2.label: SBAR to interventionalist - anterior STEMI
s1a2.env_label: Scenario 1-A handover . phone to cath lab
s1a2.env_title: Dr. Chen on the line - one shot to give the picture
s1a2.env_meta: Anterior STEMI activated. Door-to-balloon clock at 38 minutes. The interventionalist will be in the room in 8 minutes. Lead the SBAR.
s1a2.ecg_banner: ANTERIOR STEMI . D2B 38min . VERBAL ORDER NEEDED

s1a2.label: SBAR with anticoag complication
s1a2.env_label: Scenario 1-A handover . phone to cath lab
s1a2.env_title: Dr. Iyer needs the warfarin detail or she flies blind
s1a2.env_meta: STEMI confirmed. Patient on chronic warfarin, INR 3.2. The interventionalist is asking the question and you need to deliver SBAR plus the anticoag piece without burying the lead.
s1a2.ecg_banner: STEMI ON WARFARIN . INR 3.2 . VERBAL ORDER NEEDED

s1b1.label: tPA challenge - recent GI bleed
s1b1.env_title: Dr. Patel ordering tPA - patient bled 3 weeks ago
s1b1.env_meta: Stroke confirmed. Dr. Patel just ordered tPA. The chart shows GI bleed requiring transfusion 3 weeks ago - an absolute contraindication. He has not noticed. The drug is on its way up.
s1b1.ecg_banner: tPA ORDERED . CONTRAINDICATION MISSED . FIRST CHALLENGE NOW

s1b1.label: tPA challenge - major surgery 10 days ago
s1b1.env_title: Dr. Patel ordering tPA - post-op CABG day 10
s1b1.env_meta: Stroke onset 90 minutes ago. Dr. Patel ordered tPA without scrolling to the surgical history. CABG 10 days ago. Major surgery within 14 days is a contraindication. Pharmacy is preparing the dose.
s1b1.ecg_banner: tPA ORDERED . POST-OP D10 . FIRST CHALLENGE NOW

s1b1.label: tPA challenge - prior intracranial haemorrhage
s1b1.env_title: Dr. Patel ordering tPA - prior ICH on imaging
s1b1.env_meta: Stroke acute. CT shows old haemorrhagic stroke from 2022 in addition to the new ischaemic event. Prior ICH is an absolute contraindication. Dr. Patel is reading recent labs, not the imaging.
s1b1.ecg_banner: tPA ORDERED . PRIOR ICH ON CT . FIRST CHALLENGE NOW

s1b2.label: Hold firm under authority pushback
s1b2.env_title: Dr. Patel: 30 years, never had a problem
s1b2.env_meta: You raised the contraindication. Dr. Patel pushes back: 30 years experience, the bleed was minor, give the drug. He is leaning over the desk. The chart is open. The drug is at the bedside.
s1b2.ecg_banner: AUTHORITY PUSHBACK . NURSE HOLDING SAFETY POSITION

s1b2.label: Hold firm with peer pressure
s1b2.env_title: Charge nurse: just give it - he is the doc
s1b2.env_meta: Your charge nurse pulls you aside: stop slowing things down, let the doctor doctor. You know the contraindication is real. Three colleagues are watching. Hold or fold?

s1b2.label: Hold firm against time pressure
s1b2.env_title: Window closes in 18 min - Dr. Patel: just hang it
s1b2.env_meta: tPA window closing. Dr. Patel: every minute we waste arguing is a minute of brain dying. The contraindication is real. The clock is also real. He is escalating his voice.

s1c.label: Patient refuses cath - religious
s1c.env_title: Patient: 'I want to go home and pray'
s1c.env_meta: After initial stabilisation he tells you he is a Jehovahs Witness, will not accept blood, and wants to leave AMA. The cardiologist wants the cath. You are between them.

s1c.label: Capacity in question
s1c.env_meta: Patient adamantly refuses. Family says he has mild cognitive impairment but no formal dementia diagnosis. He answers some questions correctly, others not. Capacity is not obvious.

s1c.label: Advance directive conflict
s1c.env_title: DNR on file - but family wants 'everything'
s1c.env_meta: Chart shows valid DNR / DNI. Daughter just arrived from out of state, says 'You will do everything for my dad.' Patient is now drowsy and not answering. Decisions need to happen in minutes.
s1c.ecg_banner: DNR ON FILE . FAMILY OVERRIDE REQUEST

s2a.label: Wake-up stroke - onset unknown
s2a.env_title: Mrs. Faye Brennan, 67F - 'I woke up like this'
s2a.env_meta: Spouse called 911 at 0540 when she could not speak clearly. She last spoke normally at 2300 last night. She is here now at 0612. The clock is ambiguous.
s2a.ecg_banner: NIHSS 14 . LKW UNCERTAIN . tPA WINDOW QUESTIONABLE

s2a.label: Witnessed onset - 90 min ago
s2a.env_meta: Was on a video call with his daughter. She saw his face droop at 1204. He is here now at 1334. Onset is anchored.

s2a.label: Atypical - posterior circulation
s2a.env_title: Mrs. Aoife Sullivan, 73F - dizzy and clumsy
s2a.env_meta: Sudden onset vertigo, vomiting, ataxic gait. NIHSS only 4 because the standard scale misses posterior circulation strokes. Family says onset 60 minutes ago.
s2a.ecg_banner: NIHSS 4 . POSTERIOR CIRCULATION SUSPECTED . SCALE UNDER-CALLS

s2a2.label: Resident hesitating on tPA consent
s2a2.env_title: Dr. Kim freezing - NIHSS 16 and falling
s2a2.env_meta: Stroke confirmed. NIHSS 16 and worsening. Window 22 min remaining. Dr. Kim - second-year resident - is hesitating about consent because the patient cannot sign. The husband is right there as SDM. Help her see the path.

s2a2.label: BP at threshold - clinical knowledge advocacy
s2a2.env_title: BP 188/106 - Dr. Kim wants to wait and treat
s2a2.env_meta: Dr. Kim wants to start labetalol first because BP is 188/106. AHA threshold is 185/110. Treating delays tPA 10-15 min for a value already under cutoff. You have the data. She has the order pad.
s2a2.ecg_banner: BP NEAR THRESHOLD . DELAY RISK . CLINICAL CHALLENGE

s2a2.label: Window closing - resident still gathering
s2a2.env_title: Dr. Kim asking for one more imaging study
s2a2.env_meta: CT done. NIHSS 14. Window in 14 min. Dr. Kim wants a CTA before tPA. The CTA queue is 25 min. The data already supports treatment. Advocate for the patient or defer?

s2b.label: Patient in acute terror
s2b.env_title: 'Am I going to die? Am I going to die?'
s2b.env_meta: tPA running. Patient is gripping your wrist, repeating the same question. Spouse is crying in the corner. You have 60 seconds before the next vitals check.
s2b.ecg_banner: tPA INFUSING . PATIENT DISTRESSED . THERAPEUTIC PRESENCE NEEDED

s2b.label: Patient dissociating
s2b.env_title: Eyes open, not engaging, not aphasic
s2b.env_meta: NIHSS done - aphasia ruled out. She just stares past you. The neurologist says she is shutting down emotionally, not neurologically. The deficit is fear, not stroke.

s2b.label: Family conflict at bedside
s2b.env_title: Daughter and son arguing across the bed
s2b.env_meta: Adult children disagree loudly about whether mom 'wanted this kind of treatment'. Patient is right there, hearing every word, post-tPA. You need them out without alienating them.
s2b.ecg_banner: FAMILY CONFLICT AT BEDSIDE . PATIENT EXPOSED TO ARGUMENT

s2c.label: Unsafe to discharge home alone
s2c.env_meta: NIHSS down to 2. Mild left hand weakness. Lives alone in a 4th floor walk-up. No family in the city. Insurance only covers 5 days inpatient.

s2c.label: Caregiver burden invisible
s2c.env_title: Wife is the caregiver - she is exhausted
s2c.env_meta: His wife is 72, has her own COPD on home oxygen, and has been at the bedside since admission. She says 'I will manage' but you watched her cry quietly when he could not name the spoon.

s2c.label: Housing and food insecure
s2c.env_title: No fixed address - meds in fridge required
s2c.env_meta: Anticoagulant Rx requires refrigeration. Patient has been couch-surfing for 6 weeks. Discharge to 'home' is technically the shelter. Standard discharge will fail.

s3a.label: Bundle hour 1 - source unclear
s3a.env_label: Scenario 3-A . MICU . hour 1 of sepsis bundle
s3a.env_title: Mrs. Penelope Marsh, 74F - urosepsis or pneumonia
s3a.env_meta: Lactate 4.2. SBP 86 after 1L crystalloid. Source unclear - dirty UA but also new infiltrate on CXR. Bundle clock running. Antibiotic choice matters.
s3a.ecg_banner: SEPTIC SHOCK . LACTATE 4.2 . SOURCE AMBIGUOUS . BUNDLE H1

s3a.label: Neutropenic fever - chemo patient
s3a.env_meta: ANC 0.4. Temp 39.1. BP 96/58 trending down. Neutropenic fever protocol must run within 60 minutes. Single-source antibiotics will not cover.
s3a.ecg_banner: NEUTROPENIC SEPSIS . ANC 0.4 . PROTOCOL H1 . BROAD COVERAGE REQUIRED

s3a.label: Post-op day 2 - subtle decompensation
s3a.env_meta: Day 2 after laparoscopic colectomy. Reports mild fatigue. HR has crept up over 6 hours. Lactate just back at 2.8. Looks 'OK' but the trend is wrong. Early sepsis or noise?
s3a.ecg_banner: OCCULT SEPSIS SUSPECTED . LACTATE 2.8 . SUBTLE TREND

s3b.label: Senior physician dismisses concern
s3b.env_title: Attending: 'She is fine - stop calling me'
s3b.env_meta: You called Dr. Patel 20 min ago about a rising HR and falling BP. He told you to give a fluid bolus and stop calling. The trend is now worse. He is in another bay.

s3b.label: Order that does not match patient
s3b.env_meta: Order entered for vasopressor at 10x typical starting dose. The resident insists it is correct. The pharmacist is on break. The patient is being prepped for the drip now.

s3b.label: Team friction blocking action
s3b.env_title: ICU and ED teams arguing over admit
s3b.env_meta: ED says 'admit her'. ICU says 'she is not sick enough'. The patient sits in the hall on a gurney while they negotiate. You think she is septic and getting sicker.

s3c.label: Lactate rising despite fluids
s3c.env_title: Lactate now 5.8 - up from 4.2 at admission
s3c.env_meta: 3L crystalloid in. Antibiotics broad. But lactate keeps climbing. SBP only 92 with one pressor. Source control may be missing or the patient needs more.

s3c.label: Volume overload - new crackles
s3c.env_title: 4L in - new bibasilar crackles - BP still 88
s3c.env_meta: 4L resuscitation done. Lactate dropping but BP not recovering and now lungs are wet. Pressors needed. More fluid is no longer the answer.

s3c.label: AKI developing under sepsis
s3c.env_meta: Sepsis management going. But the kidneys are failing fast - oliguric, creatinine doubled, potassium climbing. Renal replacement therapy may be hours away.

s3c2.label: Wellbeing check after near-arrest
s3c2.env_title: Charge: how are you doing - that was hard
s3c2.env_meta: The patient pulled through but it was a near miss. End of shift. The charge nurse pulls you aside: how are you doing - that was a hard one. Easy to deflect. Easy to suppress. The processing matters.

s3c2.label: Wellbeing check after a hard week
s3c2.env_title: Charge: third bad shift in a row - check in
s3c2.env_meta: Third critical patient this week. Charge nurse stops you in the corridor: that is a lot - how are you. The honest answer is heavier than the script answer. What you say next sets the pattern for the next 12 months.

s3c2.label: Wellbeing check after first patient loss
s3c2.env_title: Preceptor: it is okay to feel this
s3c2.env_meta: First patient you lost despite doing everything right. Preceptor finds you in the break room. There is space here. There is time. There is no right answer. There is only what is true.
